listitem.para: Source image. The input can be a grayscale image or an RGB image.
Clip limit for contrast limiting. The default value is 3.
Output image with locally enhanced contrast. The output has the same image size as the input.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: This function applies contrast limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE). CLAHE improves local contrast while limiting the amplification of noise in nearly uniform image regions.

For a grayscale image, CLAHE is applied directly to the image intensity. For an RGB image, the image is converted to the Lab color space, CLAHE is applied to the lightness channel, and the result is converted back to RGB. This enhances contrast while preserving the color information.
If the input image is not an integer image, it is converted to uint8 before processing and converted back to the original Scilab image type after processing.

refsection.title: Examples
